返回本批次

美国劳动力参与率持续下滑

US labor force participation continues to slide

restaurant.org 作者 toomuchtodo 分数 73 评论 23 2026/04/08 03:12
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2023年11月，劳动力参与率达到62.8%，这是当年四个月内第三次达到这一水平。这些读数是 2020 年 2 月以来的最高水平（63.3%），表明更多人重新进入劳动力市场，有助于缓解疫情后的劳动力短缺问题。这对于努力招募和留住员工的餐馆和其他企业来说是个好消息，而更强劲的劳动力市场为美国经济提供了更大的弹性。
  2. 那个时期正值所谓的“大辞职”的高峰期，当时劳动力市场非常紧张，每个失业工人大约有两个工作机会。从那时起，情况已经大幅降温，让位于所谓的“美好停留”，工人和雇主都陷入了以较少工作转换为特征的停滞模式。
  3. 在当今的劳动力市场上，更多的潜在工人也转向了观望。过去一年来，民间劳动力大幅萎缩，从2025年3月的12869万人下降至2026年3月的12384万人。同时，劳动力参与率从2月的62.0%小幅下降至3月的61.9%，为2021年11月以来的最低水平。
  4. 按性别划分，过去一年左右，男性和女性的劳动力参与率均有所下降，但男性下降的速度明显更快。在男性中，参与率于 2023 年 11 月达到峰值 68.4%，为 2020 年 3 月以来的最高水平，随后于 2026 年 3 月下滑至 67.0%。这意味着过去 12 个月下降了 0.8 个百分点，是 2020 年 5 月以来的最低水平。
  5. 在女性中，劳动力参与率于 2024 年 8 月达到峰值 57.8%，此后于 2026 年 3 月小幅下降至 57.1%。与男性的大幅回落不同，女性劳动力参与率在过去一年中基本稳定，在过去 10 个读数中平均为 57.2%。

Original Summary

  1. In November 2023, the labor force participation rate reached 62.8%, marking the third time in four months that year it hit that level. Those readings were the strongest since February 2020 (63.3%), signaling that more people were re-entering the workforce and helping to ease labor shortages in the wake of the pandemic. This was welcome news for restaurants and other businesses struggling to recruit and retain employees, and a stronger labor market provided added resilience for the U.S. economy.
  2. That period coincided with the height of the so called “Great Resignation,” when the labor market was so tight there were roughly two job openings for every unemployed worker. Since then, conditions have cooled considerably, giving way to what can be described as the “Great Stay,” with both workers and employers settling into a holding pattern marked by less job switching.
  3. In today’s labor market, more potential workers have also moved to the sidelines. The civilian labor force has contracted sharply over the past year, falling from 128.69 million in March 2025 to 123.84 million in March 2026. At the same time, the labor force participation rate edged down from 62.0% in February to 61.9% in March, the lowest level since November 2021.
  4. By gender, labor force participation has declined for both men and women over the past year or so, though the pace of decline has been notably steeper for men. Among males, the participation rate peaked at 68.4% in November 2023, the highest level since March 2020, before sliding to 67.0% in March 2026. That represents a 0.8 percentage point decline over the past 12 months and the lowest reading since May 2020.
  5. Among women, labor force participation peaked at 57.8% in August 2024 and has since edged down to 57.1% in March 2026. Unlike the sharper pullback seen among men, the female participation rate has largely stabilized over the past year, averaging 57.2% across the past ten readings.

评论（默认中文）

esbranson 2026/04/08 05:03
> 2023年11月，劳动力参与率达到62.8%

2023 年 11 月， 壮年 （25-54岁）劳动力参与率为83.4%。[1]

> 与此同时，劳动力参与率从2月份的62.0%小幅下降至3月份的61.9%，为2021年11月以来的最低水平。

2026年2月和3月，黄金年龄LFPR分别为83.9%和83.8%，2021年11月为82%。[1]

壮年劳动参与率为 几乎是有史以来的最高水平 在有记载的历史中。性别差距也是有史以来最低的。[2]

[1] https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/LNS11300060

[2] https://home.treasury.gov/news/featured-stories/spotlighting...

> In November 2023, the labor force participation rate reached 62.8%

In November 2023, the prime age (25-54 years old) labor force participation rate was 83.4%.[1]

> At the same time, the labor force participation rate edged down from 62.0% in February to 61.9% in March, the lowest level since November 2021.

The prime age LFPR was 83.9% and 83.8% in February and March 2026, and 82% in November 2021.[1]

The prime age labor participation rate is just about the highest it's ever been in recorded history. The gender gap is also the lowest it's ever been.[2]

[1] https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/LNS11300060

[2] https://home.treasury.gov/news/featured-stories/spotlighting...

在 HN 中查看
drillsteps5 2026/04/08 06:12
唷。

>这些读数是 2020 年 2 月以来的最高水平（63.3%），表明更多人重新进入劳动力市场，有助于缓解疫情后的劳动力短缺问题。 劳动力短缺。疫情发生后。那件事在四年前就结束了。斯苏乌雷。

>对于 55 岁及以上的工人来说，人口因素是关键，随着越来越多的人退休，特别是自大流行以来。 知道了。 “退休”：放弃找工作，因为没有人雇用 50 岁以上的人（无论是行业还是白领）

>这些发现强化了大流行后的一个明显趋势：年轻人仍然最有可能成为劳动力的边缘。这凸显了政策制定者和企业都需要制定战略，吸引更多人重返劳动力市场——通过扩大劳动力总体供应，有助于缓解劳动力短缺问题。

“没有人愿意工作”

>随着年轻人劳动力参与率的下降，能够获得这些形成性的早期工作经验以及随之而来的基本技能的青少年越来越少，而这些经验对个人职业轨迹和更广泛的经济都具有持久的影响。

有 2 个十几岁的儿子，分别是 14 岁、15 岁和 16 岁（过去两个夏天），他们正在寻找暑期工作（美国，郊区）、零售、快餐......任何东西，真的。我让它们应用到我们周围尽可能多的地方。没有什么。

>这也与广泛流传的轶事报道相符，即今天找工作比一两年前更困难。

“轶事” 这东西谁写的？？？

Ufff.

>Those readings were the strongest since February 2020 (63.3%), signaling that more people were re-entering the workforce and helping to ease labor shortages in the wake of the pandemic. Labor shortages. In the wake of the pandemic. That ended 4 years ago. Ssuuure.

>For workers 55 and older, demographic factors are key, with more individuals retiring, particularly since the pandemic. Got it. "Retiring": giving up on finding a job because nobody hires 50+ yo (either in the trades or white collar)

>These findings reinforce a clear post pandemic trend: young men remain the most likely to be on the sidelines of the labor force. This underscores the need for policymakers and businesses alike to develop strategies to draw more of them back into the labor market—efforts that could help ease workforce shortages by expanding the overall supply of labor.

"Nobody wants to work"

>As labor force participation among young people declines, fewer teenagers are gaining access to those formative early work experiences, and the essential skills that come with them, which have lasting implications for both individual career trajectories and the broader economy.

Have 2 teenage sons, at 14, 15, and 16 (last 2 summers) they were looking for a summer job (US, suburbs), retail, fast food... Anything, really. I made them apply to as many places around us as possible. Nothing.

>It also aligns with widespread anecdotal reports that finding employment today is more difficult than it was a year or two ago.

"anecdotal" Who wrote this stuff???

在 HN 中查看
andrewclunn 2026/04/08 04:01
问题是，这些统计数据是否基于最新的人口普查数据和随后报告的就业数据？ 如果是这样，人口的变化（例如大量驱逐）可能会影响不再成立的假设分母。 也就是说，如果劳动力数据依赖于公开工作，那么也许这对分子的影响也不会那么大。 方法论在这里很重要。
Question, are these stats based on the most recent census data for population and then reported employment numbers? If so changes in population (considerable deportations for example) might effect an assumed denominator that no longer holds. That said if the labor numbers rely on above board work, then perhaps that would not impact the numerator as much either. The methodology is important here.
在 HN 中查看
toomuchtodo 2026/04/08 04:08
提交者在这里，我发帖是因为我认为这里有一些有趣的宏观经济因素在起作用。当然，移民政策也是其中的一个因素，因为餐饮业高度依赖此类劳动力。与此同时，我们仍然看到 55 岁以上的人迅速离开劳动力大军（每年约有 400 万婴儿潮一代继续退休，每月约 33 万），只剩下年轻的黄金工作年龄群体，而且这一群体仍在不断缩小。与此同时，我们看到年轻人失业率约为 6%，其中包括拥有大学学位的年轻人 [1]。

正如文章中提到的，年轻人“保持观望”，而不是从事低工资、低地位的餐馆工作（在这种情况下）。那么，谁能坚持得更久：企业和工业历来支付工人工资过低，但却“急需他们”？还是潜在的工人？因为只要美国移民受到限制，作为一家企业，无论劳动力的市场出清价格是多少，你都可以选择在这片土地上的人。

[1] 年轻人在放缓的就业市场中苦苦挣扎，即使他们拥有大学学位 - https://www.nbcnews.com/business/economy/young-men-strugglin... - 2025 年 8 月 13 日（“23 至 30 岁的男性发现学士学位无法提供与过去相同的失业保护”）

Submitter here, I posted because I think there is a confluence of interesting macroeconomic factors at work here. Certainly, immigration policy is factoring into this, as the restaurant industry is highly dependent on such labor. At the same time, we're still seeing 55+ leave the labor force rapidly (~4M Boomers continue to retire per year, ~330k/month), leaving only younger prime working age cohort, which continues to shrink. At the same time, we're seeing youth unemployment around 6%, including those with a college degree [1].

As the piece mentions, young men are "staying on the sidelines" versus engage in low wage, low status restaurant work (in this context). So, who can hold out longer: businesses and industries historically underpaying workers but "desperate for them"? Or potential workers? Because as long as US immigration is constrained, as a business, you get to pick from who is on the soil at whatever that market clearing price for labor is.

[1] Young men are struggling in a slowing job market, even if they have college degrees - https://www.nbcnews.com/business/economy/young-men-strugglin... - August 13th, 2025 ("Men ages 23 to 30 are discovering that a bachelor's degree doesn't offer the same protection from unemployment that it used to")

在 HN 中查看
panny 2026/04/08 07:38
>正如文章中提到的，年轻人“保持观望”，而不是从事低工资、低地位的餐馆工作（在这种情况下）。

你认为年轻人为什么会在餐馆工作？他们不像年轻漂亮女孩那样给小费。餐馆当然不支付生活工资。

你尝试过结束小费文化吗？如果你这样做，你可能会发现更多的年轻人愿意在那里工作，并且一旦 30% 小费的期望消失，更多的顾客愿意在那里吃饭。

>As the piece mentions, young men are "staying on the sidelines" versus engage in low wage, low status restaurant work (in this context).

Why do you think young men would work at a restaurant? They don't make tips the way pretty young girls do. Restaurants certainly don't pay a living wage.

Have you tried ending tip culture? If you did that, you might find more young men willing to work there and more patrons willing to eat there once the expectation of 30% tips are gone.

hackable_sand 2026/04/08 10:02
呃
Ew
在 HN 中查看
toomuchtodo 2026/04/08 08:31
年轻人会支持进步政策吗？他们在严峻的宏观形势下获得经济成功的唯一机会是什么？待定。
Will young men support progressive policy? Their only chance at economic success in a punishing macro? To be determined.
在 HN 中查看
bluefirebrand 2026/04/08 10:02
过去15年左右的进步政治除了要求他们成为“盟友”之外，对年轻人没有做任何事，所以年轻人在很大程度上转向右翼绝对不足为奇。

右翼承诺让他们变得伟大。这可能是一个谎言，但这比左派向他们传达的信息更好

编辑：在你误解之前，我是相当进步的。我一生都坚持投票左派。我认为这是我自己的弱点，而不是我投射给对手的东西

The last 15 or so years of progressive politics are doing nothing for young men except demand they be "allies" so it's absolutely not surprising that young men have largely moved Right

The Right is promising to make them great. It may be a lie, but it's a better message than what the Left has for them

Edit: and before you get the wrong idea, I'm pretty progressive. I have consistently voted left my entire life. This is stuff I view as weakness on my own side, not something I'm projecting on my opponents

在 HN 中查看
drillsteps5 2026/04/08 06:14
> 与此同时，我们仍然看到 55 岁以上的人迅速离开劳动力市场（每年约有 400 万婴儿潮一代继续退休，每月约 33 万）

最年轻的 X 一代 46 岁，最年长的 X 一代 65 岁。我们能停止这种“婴儿潮一代退休”的现象吗？

> t the same time, we're still seeing 55+ leave the labor force rapidly (~4M Boomers continue to retire per year, ~330k/month)

Youngest Gen X are 46, oldest are 65. Can we stop with this "retiring boomers" thing?

在 HN 中查看
toomuchtodo 2026/04/08 06:34
根据美国人口普查，婴儿潮一代的总人数要到 2029 年至 2030 年才会达到至少 65 人。一个人直到 65 岁才有资格享受医疗保险。这一点很重要，因为 55 岁以上的工作年龄人口群体目前是美国劳动力的负担。

https://www.census.gov/library/stories/2019/12/by-2030-all-b...

https://www.accurate.com/blog/how-the-retirement-boom-has-im...

年长工人是否支撑美国经济？ - https://seekingalpha.com/article/4531829-older-workers-propp... | https://archive.today/sKeyE - 2022 年 8 月 9 日

> 现在，55 岁以上的就业人数比 2000 年增加了 2000 万，相当于西班牙的全部劳动力。这种前所未有的人口/就业转变值得仔细研究。正如第二张图表所示，这一增长的部分原因是 55 岁以上的美国人人口不断增加——增加了 4200 万。 2000年，55岁及以上的人中有30%找到工作。如今，超过 37% 的人就业——55 岁以上的就业人口比例显着增加。 2000年，55岁及以上人口中只有17.6%有工作。现在这个比例是 37.5% 55 岁以上的就业者比例在 20 年内增加了 20%，这是前所未有的。如果 55 岁以上就业人口的比例保持不变，今天 55 岁以上工人的数量将仅为 1700 万。相反，数量超过 3700 万。这就提出了一个问题：为什么这么多老年工人的工作时间比 1990 年和 2000 年还要长？

（显然，老年工人继续工作，因为他们负担不起不这样做的后果，尽管我确信有一些人尽管经济上不需要，但仍继续工作）

Total Boomer cohort doesn't reach at least 65 until 2029-2030 per the US Census. One does not qualify for Medicare until 65. It matters because 55+ working age population cohort is load bearing in the US labor force at the moment.

https://www.census.gov/library/stories/2019/12/by-2030-all-b...

https://www.accurate.com/blog/how-the-retirement-boom-has-im...

Are Older Workers Propping Up The U.S. Economy? - https://seekingalpha.com/article/4531829-older-workers-propp... | https://archive.today/sKeyE - August 9th, 2022

> There are now 20 million more 55+ employed than there were in 2000, an equivalent of the entire workforce of Spain. This unprecedented demographic / employment transition is worth a closer look. As the second chart shows, some of this increase is due to the rising population of Americans over 55 years of age - an increase of 42 million. In 2000, 30% of those 55 and older were employed. Today, over 37% are employed - a significant increase in the percentage of 55+ people who are working. In 2000, only 17.6% of the 55 and older populace had a job. Now the percentage is 37.5% A 20% increase in the percentage of 55+ who are employed in a 20-year span is unprecedented. If the percentage of employed 55+ had stayed the same, there would only be 17 million 55+ workers today. Instead, there are over 37 million. This raises a question: why are so many older workers continuing to work longer than they did in 1990 and 2000?

(obviously, older workers are continuing to work because they cannot afford not to, although I'm sure there is some amount of folks who continue to work despite not financially needing to)

在 HN 中查看
drillsteps5 2026/04/08 10:08
即使您从 1965 年（而不是像某些人那样从 1961 年）开始定义 X 世代的出生年份，他们最年长的年龄已经 61 岁了。因此，55 岁到 61 岁之间的任何人都是 X 世代，而不是婴儿潮一代。而55岁以上群体的就业参与率一直在下降。这并不意味着“婴儿潮一代正在退休”，现在轮到 X 世代了。
Even if you define Gen X birth years starting at 1965 (and not 1961 as some do), their oldest are already 61. So anybody between 55 and 61 is a Gen X, not a boomer. And for the 55+ group employment participation rate has been decreasing. Which does not mean that "boomers are retiring", it's Gen X's turn now.
在 HN 中查看
alistairSH 2026/04/08 04:13
只剩下更年轻的黄金工作年龄群体，而且这一群体还在继续缩小

我不认为这确实是真的？现在，千禧一代（约占美国人口的 22%）比婴儿潮一代（约 20%）或 X 一代（约 19%）要多。 Z 世代也一样（~21%）。

或者您的意思是，无论出于何种原因，千禧一代没有参与劳动力市场？

leaving only younger prime working age cohort, which continues to shrink

I don't think that's literally true? There are more Millennials (~22% of US population) now than Boomers (~20%) or Gen X (~19%). Same for Gen Z (~21%).

Or do you mean that for whatever reason, Millenials aren't participating in the work force?

在 HN 中查看
jerlam 2026/04/08 04:22
千禧一代（以及一些 X 一代）的参与率是 2001 年以来最高的：

> 相比之下，“黄金年龄”工人（25-54 岁）的参与率相对稳定，自 2024 年 1 月以来平均为 83.6%，2026 年 3 月为 83.8%。1 月份达到 84.0%，为 2001 年 3 月以来的最高水平，目前的比率略低于最近的峰值。

Millenials (and some Gen X) have the highest participation rate since 2001:

> By contrast, participation among “prime-age” workers (25–54) has held relatively steady, averaging 83.6% since January 2024 and registering 83.8% in March 2026. It hit 84.0% in January, the highest since March 2001, with the current rate just shy of that recent peak.

在 HN 中查看
toomuchtodo 2026/04/08 04:14
> 美国劳动力正在悄然萎缩，不是因为经济疲软，而是因为人口紧缩。人口正在老龄化，进入劳动力市场的年轻工人越来越少，而壮年工人（25-54岁）的相对稳定性无法完全抵消这一差距。根据劳工统计局的预测，到 2034 年，如果参与率保持稳定，工人数量将比预期减少约 430 万人。

> 每年，美国劳工统计局 (BLS) 都会对劳动力市场进行 10 年预测。对2024年至2034年十年的最新预测显示，总体劳动力参与率（LFPR）稳步下降，从2024年的62.6%下降到2034年的61.1%，十年间下降了1.5个百分点。考虑到美国劳动力市场的规模，相对于 2024 年的参与水平，1.5 个百分点的下降意味着就业或积极寻找工作的人数减少了约 430 万人。

> 预测表明美国劳动力市场出现人口紧缩。随着人口老龄化，工人进入参与度较低的老年群体。与此同时，进入劳动力市场的年轻工人越来越少，黄金年龄参与率略有下降。尽管壮年工人的参与度很高，老年人的退休年龄也很大，但人口正在逐渐转向结构性参与度较低的年龄组。结果是整体 LFPR 缓慢但持续下降。

https://web.archive.org/web/20260407201640/https://d341ezm4i...

人口紧缩：为何劳动力参与率预计到 2034 年都会下降 - https://www.hiringlab.org/2026/04/07/why-labor-force-partici... - 2026 年 4 月 7 日

我们的劳动力人口结构正在恶化 - https://www.aei.org/domestic-policy/our-labor-force-demograp... - 2026 年 1 月 23 日

崛起的风暴：一年后 - https://lightcast.io/resources/blog/the-rising-storm-one-yea... - 发布于2026年1月15日，更新于2026年3月6日

崛起的风暴：建立一支面向未来的劳动力队伍以应对迫在眉睫的劳动力短缺 [pdf] - https://www.datocms-assets.com/62658/1761154208-the_rising_s... - 2024年

（系统思考）

> The US labor force is quietly shrinking, not because of a weak economy, but because of a demographic squeeze. The population is aging, fewer younger workers are entering the workforce, and relative stability amongst prime-age workers (aged 25-54) can’t fully offset the gap. The result, according to the BLS projections, will be roughly 4.3 million fewer workers by 2034 than would be expected if participation rates held steady.

> Each year, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) develops 10-year projections about the labor market. The latest projections for the decade stretching from 2024 to 2034 show a steady decline of the overall labor force participation rate (LFPR), from 62.6% in 2024 to 61.1% by 2034, a 1.5 percentage point drop over a decade. Given the size of the US labor market, that 1.5 percentage point decline represents roughly 4.3 million fewer people who are either employed or actively seeking work, relative to 2024 participation levels.

> The projections point to a demographic squeeze on the US labor market. As the population ages, workers move into older age groups where participation is lower. At the same time, fewer younger workers are entering the labor market, and prime-age participation rates decline slightly. Even with strong participation among prime-age workers and later retirements among older adults, the population is gradually shifting toward age groups with lower structural participation. The result is a slow but persistent decline in the overall LFPR.

https://web.archive.org/web/20260407201640/https://d341ezm4i...

The Demographic Squeeze: Why Labor Force Participation is Projected to Fall Through 2034 - https://www.hiringlab.org/2026/04/07/why-labor-force-partici... - April 7th, 2026

Our Labor Force Demographics Are Getting Worse - https://www.aei.org/domestic-policy/our-labor-force-demograp... - January 23rd, 2026

The Rising Storm: One Year Later - https://lightcast.io/resources/blog/the-rising-storm-one-yea... - Published on Jan 15, 2026, Updated on Mar 6, 2026

The Rising Storm: Building a Future-Ready Workforce to Withstand the Looming Labor Shortage [pdf] - https://www.datocms-assets.com/62658/1761154208-the_rising_s... - 2024

(think in systems)

在 HN 中查看

justonceokay 2026/04/08 04:07
